ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish, approve, and periodically review high-level organizational rules to direct the protection of informational assets.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Document and distribute formal governance policies establishing organizational rules for security planning and policy baselines.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.1 acts as a universal directive for all security policies, whereas NIST PL-1 is program-specific, requiring distinct procedures, roles, and implementation details for the planning family.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Define and allocate specific security responsibilities and authorities throughout the organization to maintain clear accountability.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Appoint a dedicated individual or governing group to oversee risk assessments, security strategies, and operational accountability.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.2 covers all security roles holistically, while NIST PM-9 focuses explicitly on establishing an enterprise-wide risk executive function connected to corporate governance.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Separate conflicting operational tasks and responsibilities to minimize risks of unauthorized changes, modification, or fraud.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Divide sensitive authorizations and duties among different individuals to prevent collusion and systemic abuse of access rights.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both controls enforce the division of operational duties to eliminate single-point vulnerabilities regarding security or transactional authorization.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Require executives to visibly support, mandate, and review the execution of information security across all organizational functions.
NIST 800-53 Control Name: Plan of Action and Milestones Process
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement institutional remediation processes driven by management to track, resource, and resolve system security gaps.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.4 emphasizes management's cultural and leadership endorsement, while NIST PM-4 mandates an explicit, documented corrective action plan framework (POA&M) for senior leaders.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish and maintain structured communication channels with relevant regulatory bodies, legal teams, and law enforcement.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Define strict guidelines and timelines for reporting security incidents and operational breaches to external public entities.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.5 focuses broadly on general ongoing relational contact with authorities, whereas NIST IR-6 mandates a systematic, structured mechanism for reporting active security compromises.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Engage with professional security associations, industry forums, and knowledge-sharing groups to track emerging threats and standards.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Participate in external bilateral or multilateral information-sharing forums to exchange threat indicators and cybersecurity defensive insights.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both framework items require active collaboration with peer networks and specialized bodies to ingest external threat intelligence and security best practices.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Collect, analyze, and process contextual information regarding operational threats to enable proactive risk mitigation and defenses.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Identify, assess, and prioritize threat indicators, systemic vulnerabilities, and likelihoods to establish comprehensive technical risk baselines.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.7 emphasizes the ingestion, curation, and dissemination of dynamic threat feeds, while NIST RA-3 focuses on formal, structured internal risk analyses utilizing that data.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Integrate information security criteria, risk evaluations, and guardrails into all phases of organizational project lifecycles.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate explicit security and privacy engineering requirements directly into the overarching development and project framework.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require that security is built into programmatic initiatives from inception rather than being bolted on post-execution.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Inventory of information and other associated assets
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Develop, document, and maintain an accurate ledger of all corporate processing assets, datasets, and supporting systems.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Create and keep updated a granular, continuous inventory tracking all physical and virtual hardware and software elements.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.9 includes intangible informational assets (intellectual property, records), whereas NIST CM-8 is highly technical, focusing primarily on hardware, virtual nodes, and physical modules.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Formulate and mandate clear guidelines detailing permitted operational behaviors and restrictions regarding corporate assets.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish, disseminate, and obtain explicit user acknowledgments regarding rules governing system usage and administrative privileges.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that end-users and administrators are bound by formal behavioral parameters when operating company-owned resources.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Ensure all workers hand back company-owned hardware, data storage, and documentation upon termination or transfer.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute offboarding checklists that require retrieval of keys, credentials, tokens, and corporate information devices from departing staff.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both manage the technical and physical reclaim of organizational properties during an employee or contractor offboarding workflow.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Categorize data assets into distinct tiers based on confidentiality requirements and potential organizational harm from disclosure.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Classify organizational processing systems and data files using explicit low, medium, or high impact metrics regarding core confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.12 focuses heavily on labeling arbitrary file contents, whereas NIST RA-2 applies a rigorous system-boundary analysis mapped to federal impact thresholds.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement an active, standardized metadata or physical tagging system to denote the sensitivity level of processed data.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Affix clear, unambiguous security markings on electronic and physical media storage components reflecting system impact levels.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require clear visual or logical indicators on data packages and storage media to guide users in appropriate handling behaviors.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish formal policies, protocols, and technical agreements to secure the transmission of data to external parties.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy system configurations and filters that restrict data movements between interconnected nodes based on defined security policies.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.14 focuses extensively on administrative mechanisms like data transfer agreements (DPAs), while NIST AC-4 mandates technical rule-based controls to block unauthorized data routing.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Govern and limit logical permissions allowing users or services to interact with specific enterprise systems and data assets.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure automated security mechanisms to enforce logical access parameters across system boundaries based on official access rules.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.15 outlines overall policy goals for access restrictions, whereas NIST AC-3 details specific technical enforcement strategies (e.g., RBAC, ABAC) within system architectures.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Control the full lifecycle of corporate identities, ensuring unique accounts are allocated to authorized entities.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Uniquely identify and authenticate all internal network actors before granting logical access to operational boundaries.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that every user profile is tied back to a singular, verifiable individual or service principal to preserve accountability.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce strict management rules regarding the creation, allocation, modification, and protection of verification secrets like passwords and tokens.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish systemic rules for managing secrets, including length, complexity, rotation schedules, and security during storage and transmit.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both control frameworks govern the security, distribution, and operational maintenance of verification factors and secrets.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Provision, adjust, and revoke application and database access privileges in strict alignment with authorization policies.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Monitor, authorize, and systematically audit user account states, changes, creations, suspensions, and termination workflows.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both address the active, ongoing lifecycle administration of user credentials and specific data privilege schemes.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Define and communicate the organization's explicit security requirements to all third-party vendors accessing enterprise environments.
NIST 800-53 Control Name: Supply Chain Risk Management Policy and Procedures
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish organizational structures, policies, and analytical models to minimize technology supply chain threats and vendor exposure.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.19 focuses on immediate business vendor agreements, whereas NIST SR-2 establishes a macro-level Supply Chain Risk Management (SCRM) program targeting deep counterfeit and provenance tracking.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Embed enforceable, contractually binding security commitments into all service level agreements and vendor contracts.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate clear security performance requirements and evaluation criteria into acquisition documentation and legal procurement channels.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require the inclusion of legal and administrative compliance clauses within purchasing contracts to enforce partner liability.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Analyze and track risks downstream through the tech supply chain, encompassing manufacturing, software development, and cloud integration.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute specialized technical assessments, code reviews, and physical integrity verifications for components throughout the procurement lifecycle.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.21 mandates basic risk management for tech supply, while NIST SR-3 outlines exhaustive, granular operations including anti-counterfeiting, tamper detection, and component tracking.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Routinely audit, assess, and manage alterations in third-party service delivery to ensure continuous adherence to contract terms.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Conduct regular technical assessments, reviews, and audits of technology suppliers to confirm adherence to security baselines.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both establish operational routines to actively audit vendor performance against security obligations rather than relying solely on point-in-time checks.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Information security for use of cloud services
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish distinct parameters for cloud configuration, shared responsibility alignment, and operational governance of multi-tenant environments.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Oversee, authorize, and audit the usage of external information systems, ensuring equivalent internal baseline controls are applied.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.23 treats cloud computing as a distinct structural scenario needing clear shared governance, while NIST SA-9 treats it under the broader category of any external hosted platform service.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Formulate comprehensive structures, team responsibilities, and playbook baselines to ensure ready execution during a cyber incident.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish operational blueprints, coordination directives, and programmatic infrastructure for enterprise incident resolution.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require the formal structural creation of incident response capabilities, communication pathways, and procedural readiness.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Assessment and decision on information security events
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Evaluate operational anomalies and security signals to accurately categorize them and declare official incident status if warranted.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute real-time identification, sorting, technical triage, and dynamic analysis of system indicators to handle active anomalies.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate a logical triage process to distinguish benign system errors from adversarial activity requiring emergency response escalation.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy containment, eradication, and system recovery activities to neutralize active threats in accordance with operational procedures.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Perform containment, coordination, tactical recovery, and internal evidence preservation when managing an operational exploit.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate technical containment steps to isolate threats and system restoration actions to return the company to steady state operations.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Analyze resolved security breaches to extract root causes, updating security parameters and training to prevent future reoccurrences.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Conduct post-incident reviews to document systemic vulnerabilities exploited and integrate changes into defensive baselines.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that security incidents serve as corrective feedback mechanisms to harden institutional defense layers.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Secure and preserve digital forensic material and chain of custody data following an incident to support legal or internal reviews.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish internal resources or expert forensic capabilities to capture artifacts, preserve volatile memory, and document breach metrics.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.28 focuses heavily on meeting standard judicial criteria for evidence admissibility, whereas NIST IR-7 highlights providing general engineering support during analytical procedures.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Maintain baseline security continuity and information controls during a disaster, emergency, or business interruption event.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Develop and execute detailed plans describing fallback procedures, roles, and technical architectures during system failovers.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.29 mandates that security protections remain functional *during* a crisis, whereas NIST CP-2 provides the exhaustive operational manual for system recovery itself.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Ensure information systems possess structural availability, redundant capacity, and failover validation to fulfill recovery time objectives.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure and establish distinct geometric backup locations equipped with proper environmental and technical safeguards.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.30 is a high-level technical capability requirement for comprehensive continuity, whereas NIST CP-6 specifies the explicit setup parameters for an alternate storage center.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Identify and map all relevant legislative mandates, contract conditions, and regulatory environments to ensure security design compliance.
NIST 800-53 Control Name: Plan of Action and Milestones Process
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Oversee corporate mapping and funding lines to ensure systemic adherence with public laws, statutory policies, and regulatory guidelines.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.31 requires cataloging the compliance matrix, while NIST maps these through the overarching enterprise Program Management (PM) controls which connect compliance directly to budget and mission.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy procedural safeguards to prevent illicit utilization, sharing, or replication of copyrighted or proprietary material.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish formal risk frameworks governing usage restrictions, licenses, and rights assessments across the enterprise.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure software and technical architectures adhere to intellectual property rules and avoid illegal utilization of code.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Secure internal operational records against unauthorized destruction, modification, or falsification in compliance with retention mandates.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Safeguard corporate records and system media from unauthorized reading or extraction using rigorous destruction techniques.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.33 centers on standard business record retention and structural integrity, while NIST MP-6 targets specific media disposal and data wiping technologies.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure technical structures and organizational policy settings to isolate, protect, and process personally identifiable data legally.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement an institutional privacy office, framework, and accountability metrics to map and monitor PII workflows.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.34 outlines security protection for personal data inside the ISMS, while NIST Rev 5 incorporates a comprehensive parallel framework of dedicated Privacy Controls (PT/PM families) that go far deeper into privacy rights.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Subject the entire enterprise security environment and control frameworks to scheduled, unbiased third-party or independent internal audits.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Appoint impartial assessors to formally audit and test systemic control efficacy to uncover systemic configuration flaws.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require an uncompromised, objective evaluation of control health by personnel independent of system management.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Compliance with policies and standards for information security
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Regularly audit individual system administrators, configurations, and network environments to confirm tight alignment with corporate baselines.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement structural automated solutions to perform perpetual configuration scanning and ongoing validation of control states.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 5.36 favors point-in-time reviews and operational check routines, whereas NIST CA-7 requires dynamic, ongoing automated continuous monitoring mechanisms.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Formulate and maintain explicit, written step-by-step instructions for managing system operations and daily IT workflows.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Develop detailed administrative roadmaps and operational guidelines for managing modifications and running core architectures.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that technical maintenance, changes, and system activities are driven by documented processes rather than informal, unverified ad-hoc habits.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Conduct systematic identity, criminal background, and historical background verifications on candidate personnel before formal hiring.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply proportional vetting and security clearance criteria to individuals prior to granting technical access to organizational boundaries.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both controls attempt to minimize internal risk vectors by verifying employee backgrounds before introducing them into systems.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce explicit contractual clauses defining a worker's personal security obligations, continuing indefinitely past their active tenure.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Secure formal user signatures on acceptable use, confidentiality, and behavioral agreements before provision of user accounts.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both manage the administrative framework that establishes legally binding security accountability on the individual worker.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Information security awareness, education and training
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deliver continuous threat briefings, phishing mock tests, and baseline security habits training to all corporate workforce units.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Disseminate broad security awareness material focused on general infrastructure hygiene, social engineering tactics, and modern exploit vectors.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both focus on human error mitigation by requiring mandatory ongoing security literacy tracks for standard users.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish a formalized administrative process to penalize, suspend, or terminate employees who intentionally violate security guidelines.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Operationalize corporate penalty structures to address deliberate non-compliance or accidental compromises of internal policies.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require clear, authoritative avenues for HR and Security to take formal corrective and punitive action against rule-breakers.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Define, communicate, and track individual security obligations that remain legally active after a resource departs the company.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Conduct exit briefings to reinforce remaining non-disclosure obligations, immediately cutting off physical and logical access paths.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both manage the final exit stage of human risk, ensuring credentials are wiped and long-term secrets remain legally protected.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Require legally binding non-disclosure pledges to protect proprietary data assets, signed by all workers and contract partners.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Require users to sign official, legally vetted confidentiality contracts prior to exposing them to internal system resources.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure information protection by wrapping system exposure in enforceable corporate non-disclosure mandates.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy specific endpoint baselines, connection protocols, and physical tracking rules to defend data processed outside formal offices.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce strict encryption, gateway architectures, and explicit management tracking for data flows originating from external networks.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 6.7 contains broad operational/physical criteria for work-from-home settings, whereas NIST AC-17 details specific encryption protocols (e.g., managed VPNs, TLS) and architectural gateway configurations.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Mandate and simplify procedures for workforce units to instantly flag observed system anomalies, bugs, or suspicious incidents.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure internal channels allowing personnel to easily report emerging system vulnerabilities or potential indicators of compromise.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both controls seek to shorten detection times by empowering standard employees to function as active security detection points.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Construct robust external structures, perimeter walls, and physical barriers around campus zones handling internal data systems.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce hard structural perimeters, facility fences, and guarded boundaries to strictly filter incoming human traffic.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both frameworks rely on structural architecture and perimeter containment to baseline the security of physical facilities.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Restrict portal entry across facilities to authorized personnel using electronic locks, badges, or manned guard stations.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Leverage badges, multi-factor electronic locks, or visitor logging procedures at perimeter access portals to grant facility entry.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require real-time validation of individual identity before permitting physical passage through facility access doors.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Design, separate, and lock inner office suites and specific equipment rooms to prevent unauthorized deep penetration into sites.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement distinct interior structural boundaries to separate open common spaces from sensitive processing cores or data rooms.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both demand multi-tiered internal physical isolation, ensuring that gaining access to the main lobby does not grant access to data centers.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy active surveillance networks, intruder alarms, and closed-circuit video setups to track building grounds continuously.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Utilize video systems, physical sensors, and centralized alarms to observe and record unauthorized movements across facility grounds.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate continuous visual or electronic monitoring of facility boundaries to instantly capture physical intrusions.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate industrial protective frameworks against natural forces like fire, flooding, seismic activity, and physical sabotage.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Integrate suppression tools, smoke detectors, water dams, and structured building reinforcement to preserve infrastructure integrity.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 7.5 merges physical and environmental threats broadly, whereas NIST splits these into highly dedicated controls for fire protection (PE-13), temperature (PE-14), and water damage (PE-15).

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish rigid operational procedures and strict monitoring parameters for personnel performing tasks inside high-security zones.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Develop authorized access logs and behavior protocols for staff specifically permitted within restricted hardware rooms.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both topic areas cover controlling personnel activities inside restricted or core mechanical layers.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce strict habits requiring workers to stow sensitive paperwork safely and enforce screen lockouts when moving away from desks.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Situate endpoint stations to block shoulder surfing, configuring automated idle locks on all display outputs.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 7.7 places emphasis on human workspace sorting habits (desks), while NIST PE-18 focuses heavily on technical configuration policies and physical hardware placement rules.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Position and isolate operational hardware elements to prevent damage from environmental factors and reduce line-of-sight exposure.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Position critical processing equipment inside protected inner structural nodes to deny unauthorized visibility and reach.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that target hardware is strategically placed in low-risk zones rather than public-facing, exposed areas.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce tracking, authorization, and technical protection models for any organizational processing gear utilized outside company facilities.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Validate and mandate baseline physical security protections for systems deployed in alternative workspaces or external locations.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both address the extension of internal security baselines to cover assets operating outside the physical protection of the main corporate office.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Manage the complete tracking lifecycle, encryption, and secure handling of portable storage drives, tapes, and disks.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Physically secure, inventory, and restrict access to physical and digital data storage units inside designated control parameters.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both frameworks prioritize tracking and guarding physical storage blocks to stop simple physical data extraction exploits.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Insulate hardware setups from power infrastructure failure by implementing backup generators, UPS systems, and steady telecom lines.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy secondary, localized power supply mechanics to maintain technical operations during baseline utility failure grid events.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 7.11 includes water and HVAC utility support under one umbrella, whereas NIST provides separate controls for backup emergency electricity (PE-11) and HVAC systems (PE-14).

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Shield and physically route power, network connectivity, and data lines to prevent active wiretapping, physical intercepts, or sabotage.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Construct physical conduits, floor pathways, and underground routings to secure communication cables against tampering or destruction.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both target protecting network and electrical copper/fiber connections from simple physical cutting or electronic intercept actions.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Perform regular maintenance on information systems and supporting structures in accordance with manufacturer parameters to ensure availability.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Schedule, authorize, and audit system hardware maintenance activities, ensuring repair workers are fully authenticated.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 7.13 focuses heavily on operational uptime and general system health, while NIST PE-12 mandates explicit pre-authorization protocols for maintenance personnel and technical tools brought on-site.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Decommission, wipe, and process hardware nodes to ensure that no residual corporate information remains on devices prior to disposal.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Utilize verified physical destruction, degaussing, or deep logical overwriting methods to eliminate remaining data on retiring media.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate advanced cryptographic wiping or physical destruction methods to prevent data leakage from obsolete, discarded hardware assets.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce strict device configuration matrices, endpoint encryption, and compliance checking on mobile and station units.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish usage policies, connection parameters, and mandatory full-disk encryption for portable technology computing platforms.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.1 encompasses stationary office workstations and corporate endpoints holistically, while NIST AC-19 is focused specifically on portable, high-risk wireless and mobile devices.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Limit, audit, and systematically control elevated account rights (such as root or domain admin) to prevent widespread exploit impacts.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce explicit technical settings to limit user and tool roles to the minimum functional access necessary for tasks.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both are designed to prevent credential amplification by locking down admin profiles and tracking their usage patterns tightly.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply file, directory, and system access blocks that filter user execution capabilities based on business role mapping.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy system access control lists (ACLs) to logically approve or deny interaction requests with system objects.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate the utilization of system enforcement points to block unauthorized read, write, and execute attempts on corporate data assets.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Isolate, protect, and restrict read and write capability over proprietary application source code repositories to prevent exploitation.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Restrict developer environments and enforce code repository controls to prevent unauthorized injections and code alterations.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.4 is a highly explicit access control requirement for source code repositories, whereas NIST addresses this as a sub-component within broad system development and supply chain engineering controls (SA family).

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate multi-factor verification mechanisms, robust cryptographic secrets, and continuous signal checks before granting entry.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Require cryptographically sound multi-factor authentication models for network, local, and administrative account verification.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both controls enforce the technical death of single-factor password authentication, mandating separate verification steps (biometrics, physical tokens).

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Monitor system capacity usage patterns, projecting storage, network, and processing requirements to maintain continuity.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure resource allocation limits and performance monitors to protect compute infrastructure against starvation and denial of service.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both prevent infrastructure exhaustion by setting active baselines and alerting on threshold breaches before service collapse occurs.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Integrate real-time behavioral and signature scanning software engines across endpoints and entryways to eliminate rogue applications.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy and automatically update server and endpoint protective modules to actively scan for and isolate viruses, trojans, and malicious macros.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate automated, systemic anti-malware operations to catch malicious files before execution inside network zones.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute regular infrastructure scanning, assess severity metrics, and implement missing security patches within established time limits.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Utilize automated vulnerability scanners to probe system interfaces, compile missing patch maps, and remediate flaw exposures.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.8 outlines the full operational management cycle of remediation and patch tracking, while NIST RA-5 details the explicit technical parameters and frequencies of automated scanning execution.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish, audit, and systematically manage secure configuration baselines for all network, software, and hardware configurations.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Develop, document, and maintain a high-fidelity baseline architecture state to guide secure system provisioning and modification.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both prevent security erosion by requiring that all systems conform to an official, tightly audited, non-default configuration state.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure database and file system features to permanently purge digital records when no longer needed, bypassing residual recovery.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Ensure that logical data allocations and memory blocks are purged completely to prevent subsequent users from reading residual bits.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both address the technical prevention of data mining exploits that target unallocated sectors or poorly deleted storage objects.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply obfuscation, pseudonymization, or masking techniques to hide sensitive metrics inside non-production or analytics databases.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement structural protections, column-level encryption, or programmatic data masking to shield data fields from unauthorized views.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.11 explicitly targets data masking for role-based viewing (e.g., hiding PII in test environments), while NIST SC-28 is broadly focused on raw cryptographic security of bulk storage volumes.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy advanced network filter modules and endpoint agents designed to automatically block outbound movement of classified corporate data.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Implement technical boundary rule configurations to prevent confidential data payloads from crossing into untrusted external networks.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.12 focuses heavily on specialized Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tooling and payload inspection, while NIST AC-4 deals with macro information flow architectures and strict zone separation models.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Generate and securely replicate data copies to isolated repositories, testing restoration routines regularly to guarantee survivability.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute scheduled system backups encompassing user data, volume states, and security metadata, ensuring offline or immutable storage.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require systematic, protected backup generation coupled with verification routines to survive ransomware or catastrophic storage crashes.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate active structural high-availability, duplicate physical lines, and network pathing options to prevent single-point downtime events.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy operational failover platforms and network routing capacity geographically separate from primary systems to sustain business operations.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.14 focuses on general clustering and local hardware high availability, while NIST CP-7 details rigorous geographic separation parameters for standby infrastructure zones.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure background operating mechanisms to capture distinct user activities, security errors, and transactional activities into logs.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Define exact events, audit parameters, and transactional anomalies that must be logged by components across system layers.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate the technical establishment of background system audit records to track configuration modifications and trace security incidents.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Analyze real-time event logs, inbound connection records, and application metrics to detect indicators of malicious behavior.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy network security monitoring, intrusion detection engines, and event analysis tools to flag unauthorized system behaviors.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both move beyond simple passive logging to require active technical monitoring frameworks that detect active intrusions.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Synchronize internal timekeepers across all system assets to a master authoritative atomic time reference to preserve forensic accuracy.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure internal system clocks to reference standard external time servers, generating synchronized forensic time stamps for all log entries.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both understand that accurate forensic timeline recreation during post-incident investigations requires exact clock alignment across all nodes.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Restrict and closely monitor powerful system utilities capable of overriding standard OS file security and control configurations.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce technical access profiles to prevent users from executing dangerous system management tools or low-level utilities.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mitigate the danger of structural bypass techniques by strictly limiting access to low-level maintenance utilities.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Installation of software on operational systems
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Configure technical barriers and endpoint restrictions to block users from executing unauthorized software files on production platforms.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce application allowlisting and block unnecessary protocols to ensure systems run only authorized software programs.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require hard administrative or technical filters (like application allowlisting) to block arbitrary, unapproved user code execution.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Govern, separate, and secure data traffic boundaries across corporate communication pathways using advanced technical protections.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy network firewalls, packet filters, and traffic management gateways at all external connection interfaces.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.20 represents a broad macro directive for general network engineering security, while NIST SC-7 explicitly outlines granular rules for interface filtering, XML inspection, and proxy placement.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish strict authorization matrices and encryption layers for specialized communication services like VPNs, DNS, and routing protocols.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Isolate network services and configure strict security parameters on internal communication gateways to block malicious traffic routing.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that ancillary network utilities and carrier protocols are locked down against hijacking or man-in-the-middle attacks.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Partition enterprise communication architecture into isolated logical zones to contain breaches and protect sensitive backend records.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce explicit internal subnetting, VLAN separations, and logical isolation zones between corporate and DMZ assets.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require network micro-segmentation to ensure a compromise in a public-facing web server does not grant flat lateral access to database segments.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Deploy proxy content filters to automatically block browser connections to dangerous, malicious, or unapproved web domains.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Monitor and intercept user outbound web traffic, utilizing domain filters to block communication with command-and-control servers or malware drops.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate the active filtering of web traffic to minimize user exposure to drive-by malware downloads and social engineering sites.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Mandate high-grade encryption algorithms, key distribution mechanisms, and secure lifecycle practices across all corporate architectures.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Leverage validated mathematical cryptographic modules to protect the confidentiality and integrity of classified data blocks.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure data confidentiality by mandating approved cryptographic standards (e.g., FIPS 140-validated modules for NIST) across system layers.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Establish mandatory secure software development guidelines, code reviews, and testing milestones for all in-house applications.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Govern software creation processes using an structured lifecycle framework that mandates continuous security engineering reviews.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both demand that application security features are integrated throughout the development lifecycle rather than being addressed ad-hoc near release.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Analyze and specify individual security criteria, user input validations, and threat profiles when engineering application systems.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Incorporate explicit technical security specifications and feature requirements directly into design and software acquisition phases.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both ensure that application architectures are designed from inception to handle malicious inputs and enforce specific security capabilities.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply verified structural architecture principles, including zero-trust design, defense-in-depth, and fail-secure settings during system implementation.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply system engineering blueprints based on defense-in-depth, complete mediation, and isolation models to reduce exploit surfaces.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both mandate moving past ad-hoc security configurations, forcing engineering teams to apply foundational security architecture patterns.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Train developers and enforce specific coding practices to block buffer overflows, injection exploits, and logic bugs.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Require developers to perform code analysis, prevent known software vulnerabilities, and follow structured coding standards.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both target vulnerability elimination at the source by mandating secure coding practices and preventing common security flaws (e.g., OWASP Top 10).

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Security testing in development and acceptance
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Subject modified applications to static, dynamic, and penetration testing routines prior to pushing code into production status.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Execute explicit vulnerability scanning, regression verification, and security quality checks before authorizing product rollout.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require rigorous pre-deployment technical validation to identify design flaws and code openings before code exposure to live threats.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Supervise, audit, and mandate strict compliance with internal secure coding standards when utilizing external software vendors.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Apply explicit supply chain checks and developer vetting rules on external third parties building custom software elements.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Partial. ISO 8.30 centers on monitoring contract software developers, while NIST SA-12 provides a far broader supply chain tracking model including third-party code provenance, tampering, and legal tracking.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Separation of development, testing and production environments
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Isolate development, testing, and production environments logically and physically to prevent untested code or active bugs from impacting production networks.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Enforce distinct access boundaries and execution limits to isolate development sandboxes from live production databases.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both controls prevent configuration mixing, ensuring that development work and software testing occur completely separate from live transaction spaces.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Subject all systems modifications, environment changes, and updates to formal approval, documentation, and regression testing reviews.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Coordinate, analyze, and track physical and logical updates to the environment using a formal configuration board review mechanism.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require rigorous change control procedures to prevent undocumented, rogue, or untested changes from undermining security baselines.

ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Protect and sanitize testing data, avoiding the utilization of live production PII datasets inside non-production environments.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Restrict the use of operational production data in test environments, requiring sanitized or synthetic datasets.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both prevent data leakage by prohibiting developers from copying live customer PII or transaction histories into less-secure testing sandboxes.

ISO 27001:2022 Control Name: Protection of information systems during auditing testing
ISO Control Summary (Paraphrased): Coordinate and schedule security assessment activities carefully to minimize operational performance impacts and prevent system disruptions.
NIST Control Summary (Paraphrased): Manage active control assessment and configuration checking routines so they do not interrupt system operations or degrade core system performance thresholds.
Completeness & Gap Analysis: Full. Both require that automated scanning or active manual audit verifications are managed to protect live production environments from accidental downtime.
